rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical vicinal fluorosulfonylation of styrenes using arylsulfonyl hydrazides and Et3N·3HF via a radical-polar crossover pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arylsulfonyl hydrazide to arylsulfonyl radical (with loss of N2, 3H+, 3e-); subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzyl radical adduct to a benzyl cation
electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the arylsulfonyl hydrazide through consecutive SET (losing 3H+, 3e-, N2) to form an arylsulfonyl radical, which adds to the styrene to give a benzyl radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation that is captured by fluoride from Et3N·3HF, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: (1-(2-phenylcyclopropyl)-vinyl)benzene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened fluorosulfonylation product (40) obtained in 15% yield, supporting a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: N-methyl-N-phenylmethacrylamide
probe_reagents.observation: Radical cyclization product (42) obtained in 42% yield in absence of Et3N·3HF, supporting arylsulfonyl radical generation

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of ArSO2NHNH2 and styrene
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of arylsulfonyl hydrazide at 1.70 V (vs Ag/AgCl); styrene at 2.52 V (shifted to 2.36 V with (NH4)2HPO4), indicating sulfonyl hydrazide is preferentially oxidized

notes: Authors propose that anode-adsorbed HPO4^2- may H-bond with hydrofluoride to enhance nucleophilic fluorination, accounting for the beneficial additive effect of (NH4)2HPO4. Unactivated olefins (cyclohexene, 1-octene) fail, attributed to instability of the corresponding carbocation, further supporting the radical-polar crossover (cation) pathway over a pure radical chain.
